items.prompt: Which statement best matches how this chapter defines a dataset?
choices: Any raw log stream from a production system
A curated collection of data, often as records and attributes, prepared for a purpose
Only CSV files with numeric columns
A trained machine learning model checkpoint
items.explain: The chapter treats a dataset as curated for analysis, with scope, structure, and ideally documentation—not merely raw operational logs.

items.prompt: In Example 1.1 (sales CSV), what is one record?
items.explain: Each row is one observation—one sale. Columns are attributes of that sale.

items.prompt: A dataset must always be a flat table of rows and columns.
items.explain: Example 1.4 (housing JSON) shows nested, semi-structured forms; images and text are also datasets when curated for a purpose.

items.prompt: In one sentence, what is the difference between a record and an attribute?
items.rubric: Record = one observation/row; attribute = one property/column of that observation.
items.sample_answer: A record is one observation (row); an attribute is one property that describes that observation (column).
